description: Regression: an AI PILE_IN logged '(N models moved)' but the tokens never moved on the board. AIPlayer._execute_next_action emits ai_action_taken (-> Main._on_ai_action_taken) BEFORE routing the action, so a synchronous update_unit_visuals() synced tokens to the PRE-move state (a no-op) and nothing re-synced after the phase applied the move (the FightPhase.pile_in_preview signal has no listeners). This scenario drives the REAL Main._on_ai_action_taken for a PILE_IN in that exact order, then applies the model move the phase would apply, and asserts the on-screen token catches up to the new GameState position. Fix: defer the token sync to end-of-frame so it reads post-move state.
